RaceName: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Classified Stakes (RE Series)
Spotlight: Over two years since the last of his three wins; yet to shine in 2026 but he drops into a 0-50 classified for the first time and he can't be discounted.

RaceName: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Classified Stakes (RE Series)
Spotlight: Two 1m2f wins this year, the latest a fast-ground handicap at Leicester in May; respectable efforts since without hitting quite the same heights; should give a good account.

RaceName: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Classified Stakes (RE Series)
Spotlight: Scrambled home in a 1m6f handicap here (good to firm) last month; no threat in an Epsom handicap since; drops in trip and wouldn't want this to become tactical.

RaceName: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Classified Stakes (RE Series)
OwnerName: M L W Bell Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Comfortable win in a similar event at Wolverhampton (1m4f) in February; runner-up in Yarmouth events (1m2f/extended 1m3f) twice this month; another big run expected.

RaceName: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Classified Stakes (RE Series)
OwnerName: Gee Gee's Racing & Hold My Beer
Spotlight: Modest form in maiden/novice company at up to 8.6f; tailed off in a 1m2f handicap at Leicester 17 days ago; drops in grade with cheekpieces added but plenty to prove.

RaceName: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Classified Stakes (RE Series)
Spotlight: Second of six, beaten over 3l, in a similar event at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm; cheekpieces first time) last month; modest hurdle form nine days ago; not fully exposed on the Flat.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Modest form in two sprint runs this summer; no appeal.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ran green when a well-beaten fifth of six in a 5f fillies' novice here (good to soft) in October; sold out of Ed Walker's stable for 11,000gns later that month; hooded for stable/seasonal debut.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 50-1 for her debut at Kempton (7f, AW) nine weeks ago, weakening in the final furlong; drop in trip/addition of a tongue-tie needs to spark a jolt of progress; worth a market check all the same.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Her debut run at Bath (5.7f, good) was encouraging but she hasn't kicked on in two subsequent runs (both on AW), the latest at Wolverhampton (6f) last week; needs more.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well-related filly who fared best of the newcomers, behind a long odds-on favourite, on her Windsor debut (6f, good to firm) early last month; reared as the stalls opened when fourth of five at Salisbury (7f, good to firm) ten days ago; now tried in a hood; capable of better.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Modest form in two sprint runs this summer; minor handicaps should prove more suitable.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: She has been consistent rather than progressive in novice events; return to sprinting should suit and she holds place claims once again.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: She has improved her RPR with each of her three starts, all over 7f, keeping on for third at Yarmouth six weeks ago; drop in trip a slight niggle but she's open to further progress.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Now Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who hit a flat spot then rallied well in first-time cheekpieces over C&D (good to firm) most recently, getting up close home; may build on that novice win and show further progress in the retained headgear; not disgraced at Royal Ascot the time before; respected.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Now Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Possibly going to prove an ordinary sort, having produced best effort in a seller on penultimate start; however, ran respectably (fourth of seven) in C&D nursery latest and looks a reasonably solid contender kept to this scenario; Warren Fentiman takes off a useful 3lb.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Now Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Creditable second off top weight in Class 6 event at Ffos Las (5f, good; nursery debut) last Tuesday, backing up the form of his 66-1 placed effort a couple of starts earlier; upped in grade but receives weight all round this time, albeit from rivals who already have course form.

RaceName: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark for new stable when taking a 1m2f handicap here (good to firm) 19 days ago; a 3lb rise shouldn't be an issue but he wouldn't want this to get tactical.

RaceName: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Consistency has been a major issue for him but he has the ability to win at this level; this will be the longest trip he has tackled but it looks worth trying.

RaceName: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Ran well from the front here (1m, good to soft; blinkered first time) in May but she has followed it with two heavy defeats; down in the weights but she is not a solid option.

RaceName: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Comes here chasing a hat-trick after narrow 1m2f wins on fast ground at Beverley and Redcar (cheekpieces first time); a 6lb penalty for the latter success (on Sunday) asks a stiffer question, but he could enjoy the run of the race and looks the one to beat.

RaceName: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Just a glimmer of promise in her five starts, the latest her handicap debut at Windsor (1m, good to firm) five weeks ago; still has time to do better but she needs more for the additional yardage.

RaceName: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: His two wins have come over 1m at Bath, the more recent coming off a 3lb lower mark in April; runner-up on his last three starts and no obvious reason why he wouldn't be in the thick of it.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Won on his third bumper start for Andy Slattery when making all at Roscommon in August; well held on yard debut in Listowel maiden hurdle the following month, and ran like he needed his comeback at Tramore in late May.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed all three bumper starts for John McConnell, including Cheltenham third in October where he was a neck off a Skelton horse who has won three hurdles since and is now rated 137; after six months off, unplaced in a Kilbeggan bumper on stable debut; good third of 17 on hurdling debut at Listowel in late May; not much of note behind him, but winner had landed a bumper impressively and runner-up was sixth in the Asoct Stakes next time.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Valirann gelding off the mark on seventh starts in points in May, when with John Edward Walsh; hood on for stable/rules debut.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Newcomer by Aizavoski cost 12,500euros as 3yo; second foal; dam unraced sister to bumper and useful jumps winner Shoal Bay.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Tailed off in soft-ground bumper here last year; was shaping well on hurdling debut at Clonmel when knuckling on landing two out, fourth at the time and with running left to give; well beaten in Roscommon novice latest, but the three ahead of him were all previous winners; place chance.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Phoenix Of Spain half-brother to five Flat winners was second in 1m2f maiden at Ripon last year, when trained by the owner's son; well beaten in three starts on the Flat in Ireland; tongue-tie fitted for hurdling debut; can be keen.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Beaten favourite but not a bad run in first bumper at Galway in October, beaten 9l; not as good next time and, for one from champion trainer's yard, significant that she was sent off 22-1 when beaten 42l on hurdling debut at Roscommon

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Big prices and well beaten so far, although the Sligo maiden in which she was beaten 22l on her penultimate start has produced three winners.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Well beaten in a bumper and maiden hurdle.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Diamond Boy mare cost 50,000euros at three; half-sister to winners Scorpion's Revenge (bumper/useful US 2m2f hurdle), Arthur Fonzarelli (2m1f hurdle) and Neigh Botha (bumper and 2m/2m1f hurdle/chase); dam unraced half-sister to Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Native River.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Modest on the Flat, rated just 34; can only be watched on hurdling debut.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 40s, never invoved when beaten 30l on debut in maiden hurdle at Sligo.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 33s, hampered and unseated rider at the second at Roscommon; will need this experience.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 20s when ridden by Harry Cobden in Roscommon maiden hurdle, beaten 43l; will need more time.

RaceName: O'Hare's Bar Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Nothing so far Flat or hurdles.

RaceName: Hospitality At Nottingham Racecourse Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Disappointed when sent off favourite for her stable debut/reappearance at Newmarket (7f. good; first-time hood) last month, but made no mistake at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) 19 days ago; 3lb higher and meets some less exposed rivals, though the sole 4yo beat four 3yos in this race last year.

RaceName: Hospitality At Nottingham Racecourse Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: A bit up and down in her four starts, but she did it well enough when successful at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) last month, beating Luna Celeste by just under a length; 3lb rise fair enough, though not quite sure how strong the form is with the third and fourth both finishing out of the frame since.

RaceName: Hospitality At Nottingham Racecourse Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Hooded all starts; dead-heated in her sole outing as a 2yo and back to form when beaten a head at Sandown (1m, good) last month; up 4lb, but the form of that race is working out well; respected.

RaceName: Hospitality At Nottingham Racecourse Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: Julie & David Martin, Dan Hall & Partner
Spotlight: 2-3 since handicapping, with both wins coming at around 1m on good to firm ground at Hamilton and Beverley; high on list despite a 4lb higher mark.

RaceName: Hospitality At Nottingham Racecourse Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark with a narrow success in a Leicester maiden (6f, good to firm) in May and was beaten less than a length by Darzah on handicap debut at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) last month; now 2lb better off with her old rival, but a couple of those behind haven't boosted the form since.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Flat winner has a kink in him, and has twice run out, but solid seconds last twice and there was good money for him last time when clear of the remainder at Sligo, beaten around 2l by 115-rated rival; ground suits and this is a good opportunity.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Affinisea half-brother to 2m3f/3m hurdle winner Cosmo Renfro; dam unraced half-sister to useful 2m4f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Shang Tang, out of bumper/2m hurdle winner.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Best run was when beaten 27l in Roscommon maiden hurdle; tailed off other starts under rules.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Godolphin cast-off related to Group winners; decent maiden form last summer for Matt Smith, including  narrow defeat in 10.5f maiden at Down Royal, and raced off 78 when last seen in that sphere (Whats New is 62 on the Flat); lame on hurdles debut; after seven months off, beaten 20l at Sligo in May where jumping was novicey late on.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Pulled up in a point and only got as far as the first flight of hurdles here in May.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Second in Roscommon bumper last year; two mishaps after, including a fall when out of contention on only hurdles run; beaten 20l in bumper latest.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Fancied for two points but failed to complete; 30l fourth over 2m4f on hurdling debut at Limerick gives him something to build on.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Best of four point runs was on debut when 4l off useful type; tailed off in bumper latest.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Unplaced in two bumpers before hurdling debut sixth at Tramore; 2-1 fav at Kilbeggan, travelled well to challenge but stuck his head up on the run-in and was inclined to lug in behind the winner; that raises a concern and bit to find with Whats New on the figures.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Waldgeist gelding is a sibling of nine winners, notably smart hurdler Colonel Mustard; beaten favourite all starts; one-paced in two bumpers over farther; no extra in fifth on hurdling bow at Roscommon, after being messed about by a loose horse and making mistakes; a less eventful contest should see a better performance in a first-time tongue-tie.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
OwnerName: Ms Margaret Nevin & Ms Alana Insole
Spotlight: Homebred Jukebox Jury mare whose dam was useful 2m4f/2m5f hurdle winner for Willie Mullins; fine run when 100-1 in 2m maiden hurdle here, beaten 17l and splitting two subsequent winners; gets an extra furlong here, but will probably want 2m4f before long; still, should go well in first-time tongue-tie.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Big prices and has shown little in bumpers and over hurdles; new headgear combination.

RaceName: West "Howya" Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Rules debut for Berkshire half-sister to smart staying jumper Salvador Ziggy; pulled up in two point runs with a week of each other last October.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th Oct Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins and a second from three visits to Nottingham, all over this trip on fast ground; 1lb higher than for his latest second; one to take seriously.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th Oct Handicap
Spotlight: Unplaced in six runs for David O'Meara, but he's tumbling down the weights and he showed more at York (6f, good to firm; visored first time) ten days ago; this is a drop in class and something bigger is brewing.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th Oct Handicap
OwnerName: Ged Mason, Sir Alex Ferguson, Jim McGoff
Spotlight: Second to Jenever over C&D last October but held by that rival at the revised weights; his form has dipped on his last two starts but he's capable of a prominent showing.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th Oct Handicap
Spotlight: Like many from the yard he has had a quiet year but he has dropped down the weights and is now 6lb lower than for his C&D win last October; quite possible the winners will start flowing for Jessica Macey now and he's of some interest.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th Oct Handicap
Spotlight: First and second in two 6f novice runs on AW over the winter; no impact in three handicap runs, including her turf debut 17 days ago, and may need further help from the assessor.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th Oct Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Newmarket novice (6f, good) last May, his third start, but beaten 11 times since including all four runs for John Butler; his penultimate second at Thirsk was a good run though and when down the field at York last time it was a Class 4; not out of it.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th Oct Handicap
Spotlight: His 6f AW win for George Scott in March came off this mark; unplaced in both runs for his new stable and others have more pressing claims.

RaceName: Construction Day 14th Oct Handicap
Spotlight: Better on AW than turf but handicapped accordingly; not beaten far at Wetherby on his penultimate start but this is a better race and he has since finished down the field at Wolverhampton.

RaceName: David Heneghan Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Had been mixing Flat and hurdling action to useful effect before an off-day at this venue in May; hard to fancy after another poor run at Limerick.

RaceName: David Heneghan Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Shock win when bolting up at 40-1 off 93 at Cork in April (2m, yielding); mid-field off revised mark at Limerick later that month, when testing ground may not have suited; unplaced favourite in a novice contest at Downpatrick last month.

RaceName: David Heneghan Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won 2m1f Clonmel handicap in April (yielding), followed that with a couple of ordinary runs off revised mark; good chance now following a return to form with Wexford second; yard has been among the winners lately and tongue-tie added.

RaceName: David Heneghan Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Some promise in two maiden hurdles in 2024 but was disappointing when favourite on return at Tipperary early last season; pulled up on handicap debut last August; absent since; from a top stable, so not ruled out

RaceName: David Heneghan Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a Downpatrick maiden hurdle for Gordon Elliott in April 2024; Galway fifth (2m, good to yielding) last October was best effort since joining this yard; should be better for his run at this track in May.

RaceName: David Heneghan Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has shown his best form at Clonmel, but was pulled up at that venue on his most recent start; previous fourth at Limerick was no more than satisfactory.

RaceName: David Heneghan Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ended last Flat season with a respectable fifth on handicap debut in a 25-runner 1m2f Curragh race on heavy ground; fit from hurdling before beginning new Flat campaign with an encouraging fifth at Leopardstown; not as good at that venue last time, but may improve on previous hurdling form.

RaceName: David Heneghan Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Some promise in 2024 maiden runs; tailed off at Listowel in May on first run after a long layoff; handicap debut now.

RaceName: David Heneghan Memorial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Took a small step in the right direction on seasonal debut at Sligo, much more required; 2lb wrong but useful 5lb claimed.

RaceName: RMG Proudly Supports Teenage Cancer Trust Handicap
Spotlight: Winless on turf since 2021 but showed more on his second run back from a break last time, is now 2lb lower and this is easier than the handicaps he's used to contesting; tends to be held up and a speed-favouring 5f in a small field may not be optimal but he's capable.

RaceName: RMG Proudly Supports Teenage Cancer Trust Handicap
Spotlight: York win last summer came off 15lb higher, which shows how things have gone in the meantime; can miss the break (including in first-time headgear latest; now removed) but this race is easier than he's used to; career-low mark.

RaceName: RMG Proudly Supports Teenage Cancer Trust Handicap
Spotlight: Has not had much luck and continues to give the impression that this mark, which is just 2lb higher than when winning at Windsor in the spring, is within range; another who tends to be ridden from off the pace and he wouldn't want a messy affair.

RaceName: RMG Proudly Supports Teenage Cancer Trust Handicap
OwnerName: Come On Over Syndicate & A H Yates Ltd
Spotlight: No problem with 7f last backend and he has been running over 6f this summer, latterly when making all at Doncaster early in the month; came away with a subsequent winner that day and finally getting off the mark should have helped confidence; a 4lb rise looks fair.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins and two seconds to his name from ten runs over C&D but he's struggled this year; absent for ten weeks since hanging right on the home turn over C&D and others make more obvious appeal.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Big prices and well beaten in three runs over 1m2f to date; possible she'll improve for the new trip but she'll have to off this opening mark; jockey rode his first Flat winner early in the month (had two over hurdles as well); market useful.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Dam was a miler and he's not bred for this sort of trip; performed below market expectations all four runs since handicapping and, while his trainer-jockey combination will draw some in, stamina is an obvious concern.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Long time since he won on the Flat and he had lost his way when last seen; the change of yard will need to spark a revival and there's nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Hit with a sharp rise in the weights after running away with a 0-55 here last backend and has struggled since, including in one go over this far; has moved yards yet again (new trainer also runs Shopaholic) which needs to have helped.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Dual 1m4f winner here in April, latterly off a 2lb lower mark; back in form without the usual cheekpieces (on again tonight) on turf last time and won't mind being back on Tapeta; goes on the shortlist.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: His two turf wins last summer came off higher marks, including in an amateurs' handicap; moderate form this year and the blinkers, worn once before when pulled up over fences, are given another try.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Strong in the market on her first run in almost a year when third at Yarmouth a fortnight ago; comfortably held that day in a race than didn't pan out her way and she is entitled to improve, but has stamina to prove.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: His three wins have come at Brighton and he has a patchy record on Tapeta; has been running poorly and doesn't make much appeal.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Infrequent winner who's been this far only once, when not seeing it out; pulled hard for his new yard when third at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft) last month, then clipped heels and unseated early 11 days later; needs to settle better.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Blew the start before finishing a remote last on his comeback two months ago; will need a big step forward.

RaceName: RBC Brewin Dolphin Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Plenty of experience over hurdles and fences in France, had nine runs as 4yo; after a break, was well-backed winner of 2m maiden hurdle at Limerick and third won by a street lately; tackles good ground for the first time, but Doctor Dino's progeny are rarely troubled by it, nor is his talented brother and stablemate Zurich.

RaceName: RBC Brewin Dolphin Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a bumper in France, but had proved frustrating over hurdles until off the mark on ninth start in this discipline when making all over this trip in a Sligo maiden; runner-up has leading claims in 5.06 on this card; son of It's Gino with poster-boy looks and should make a nice chaser in time.

RaceName: RBC Brewin Dolphin Rated Novice Hurdle
OwnerName: M R D Fleming & G Bruce & J P Ryan & Miss G Ryan
Spotlight: Up front throughout when winning Sligo handicap over this trip; effectively 10lb higher, but further progress is likely.

RaceName: RBC Brewin Dolphin Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Long-standing maiden in both codes; second in claiming hurdle at Sligo two runs back; 7lb wrong at the weights and hard to fancy in this company.

RaceName: RBC Brewin Dolphin Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Unplaced all eight starts and all of 31lb wrong at the weights; would need to start now.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Horse Feeds "Carnarvon" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Novice winner who is 0-6 in handicaps but ran encouragingly at Chepstow (6f; finished well from rear) most recently and is now a stone below her opening mark; interesting dropped to 0-55 level.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Horse Feeds "Carnarvon" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner who is only 1-22 on turf but has been placed a few times since returned to this sphere, runner-up at Leicester most recently; fighting chance off current mark in this field.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Horse Feeds "Carnarvon" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden prior to building up a good strike-rate (3-6) on AW this year; however, disappointing when favourite last time and has something to prove back on turf.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Horse Feeds "Carnarvon" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Inconsistent this season but posted one of his better efforts last time; dual winner at Salisbury, including narrowly under Freddy Tett in this race last year; respected off a workable mark.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Horse Feeds "Carnarvon" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Scored at Lingfield in most recent turf start; record at Salisbury features two wins (2023/2024) and a close fourth in this contest 12 months ago; again has frame possibilities.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Horse Feeds "Carnarvon" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Campaigned mostly at sprint distances; scored three times for previous yard but has inconsistent results for current stable; something to prove back over 7f.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Horse Feeds "Carnarvon" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Has gained all wins over 6f but went very close (off 3lb higher) in this race last year, albeit was in better form at the time; admittedly may prove resurgent with Brighton reappearance under his belt.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Horse Feeds "Carnarvon" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: 1m AW winner in 2024 for previous yard; missed 2025 and trailed home last of five at Salisbury (6f) ten days ago on reappearance; still has broadly poor form on turf.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Horse Feeds "Carnarvon" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Campaigned mostly on AW but ran well (close fifth off 4lb higher) in this contest 12 months ago and that form puts her in the picture; possibly needed latest run (back from a break).

RaceName: Highclere Castle Horse Feeds "Carnarvon" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Inconsistent maiden who has raced mostly on AW; posted one of her better efforts last time; no certainty to back up that form, especially returned to turf.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: He was back up to 1m (turf) when a well-backed favourite last time, but looked to have no excuses in finishing last of six; trainer has won with three of his last five runners, two at big prices, but this gelding has stamina to prove over this far.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: Made it 3-3 over C&D when winning off a 3lb lower mark five weeks ago (ridden from off the pace by Jack Callan that night and may be again from stall 13); stable flying this month, with seven winners from its last 15 runners at the start of the day, and he holds good claims.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: Dual winner at around 1m early in the year, latterly off 1lb lower, who's been this far only once (C&D last backend), when well held; offered more last time but stamina remains a worry.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: Held off his revised mark on turf since two AW wins, including here, in April; wins have all come over shorter but he stays this far and is one to consider back on Tapeta.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Tapeta nursery the only time he's run on the AW; the change of surface is the main hope, with him having struggled in three turf runs since coming back from nine months off.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: Struggled since handicapping this year and he blew the start at Wetherby last month before pulling too hard on Tapeta a fortnight ago; goes beyond 1m for the first time and will need to settle better upped from 7f.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: Five-time course winner who failed to enhance a good record for Rossa Ryan when fluffing the start here a fortnight ago; has a competitive mark, though, and holds each-way claims under Pat Cosgrave.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: Sole handicap win came off this mark, the only time she's run on Tapeta, here (8.7f) last November; offered more on turf last time and she's one to consider back on the AW.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: Now 2-3 over C&D having blown the start and hit a bit of traffic a fortnight ago; also had excuses here previously and she'd been consistent prior to that, but she has to prove this mark is within range.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: Better with each of his three runs for this yard and wasn't beaten far at Kempton a fortnight ago, despite hanging right; stays this far and is quite happy on Tapeta, so he's one to consider.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: Dual Carlisle winner who's recorded his best efforts on turf; showed the right attitude off 2lb lower last time but will need to at least match that form in this competitive handicap.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
Spotlight: This has always been his time of year and he's a dual C&D winner who's back on a handy mark; it wouldn't be a shock if he popped up.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap
OwnerName: Making Headway Racing And K Scott
Spotlight: Very rarely goes this far and it's a long time since he won at higher than 0-55 level; always a chance he'll miss the break too and he's some way down the pecking order.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ended his time with Fergal O'Brien with back-to-back hurdle wins at Taunton and Chepstow in March/April 2024; after a long absence, had two Flat runs (including a satisfactory display at this venue) before being sent back over hurdles at Sligo nine days ago; made all and has a good chance despite 5lb rise.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has recorded three of his five hurdle starts at Cartmel; below his best there last time; overall Irish form is unconvincing.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 2-14 over hurdles; returned from a long layoff with a decisive Flat win at Navan in March (1m6f, heavy); well beaten off this hurdles mark at Roscommon last month; capable of better, appears to be ground-versatile.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Came back from a long layoff to start last season's campaign with back-to-back handicap wins at Navan (soft-to-heavy) and Leopardstown (yielding); beaten favourite in two novice hurdles in January; perhaps some doubt about how he will handle the ground.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Returns from an 11-month layoff on a hat-trick after back-to-back victories at this venue and Bellewstown, both on good ground, last August; has gone up a total of 13lb; further progress is feasible.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a Tipperary maiden hurdle last year; pulled up in a chase at Wexford last month; had an excuse (badly hampered four out) when well beaten at last year's Galway festival on his only handicap hurdle outing.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has lost her way since winning a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle last August; beaten 24l in mares' handicap hurdle at this venue

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Had shown little, Flat or hurdles, before running way above expectations (300-1) in a conditions hurdle at this venue in May; another fair run in a Downpatrick maiden hurdle; down the field in a Flat handicap at Limerick last time; new headgear combination.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Sole win for Ross O'Sullivan came in a Thurles maiden hurdle (2m, good) the season before last; weak handicap form last season; hard to make a case for him on stable debut.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Mrs Helen Mullins & M J Lomax
Spotlight: No success since a juvenile maiden hurdle win at Fairyhouse in November 2024; has dropped a long way in the ratings from an opening mark of 111 to 94; should enter calculations based on a third placing at Clonmel in April.

RaceName: Kinross British EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Justified market support with neck success at Ffos Las (6f, good) to contribute to his stable's excellent strike-rate (5-9) with 2yos on turf this term; the clear pick of the runners with experience and represents last year's winning connections.

RaceName: Kinross British EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 105,000euros yearling; by Invincible Spirit; sibling to four winners, notably Light Blush (Listed-placed 7f; 96) and Happy Hadeda (6f AW 2yo; RPR 75); dam 1m2f winner (including Listed; 105); one of three newcomers who add interest to this race.

RaceName: Kinross British EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Always towards the rear in 6f contest at Newbury where he was a 40-1 shot; perhaps one for handicaps later on.

RaceName: Kinross British EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 55,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; by Havana Grey; sibling to two winners, notably Star Of Lady M (prolific 5f-6f including 2yo/Listed; RPR 108); dam 7f AW winner (80), from family of top-class 1m2f-1m4f performer Fantastic Light; yard's 2yo strike-rate this season bodes well; respected debutant.

RaceName: Kinross British EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 36,000euros foal, 40,000gns yearling; by Space Blues and first foal of 1m6f Flat (RPR 72) and 2m/2m1f hurdle winner and daughter of Italian 1m2f Group 1 winner; owner is also represented by form pick Soldier Of The Sea; heed the market signals.

RaceName: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C-D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Half-sister to six winners, but never figured when eighth of 11 on her Newbury debut (6f, good to firm; 18-1) 12 days ago; related to AW winners, but improvement needed.

RaceName: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C-D) (GBB)
Spotlight: March foal by Palace Pier; 30,000gns yearling; closely related to US 1m/8.5f turf winner Principled Stand, half-sister to winners Forever Blue (6f 2yo/7f; RPR 90) and Savrola (1m-2m including AW; 85); dam 1m 2yo/11.6f Listed winner (RPR 97), half-sister to 1m4f Group 1 winner; stable only has the occasion winning 2yo newcomer; market informative.

RaceName: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C-D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Well held in turf maidens at Windsor (6f, good to firm) and Doncaster (7f, good to firm); tongue-tie is now given a go but she may be one for nurseries; stable also runs the newcomer Senorita Luna.

RaceName: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C-D) (GBB)
OwnerName: Mohamed Rashid Hamdan Al Saadi & Partner
Spotlight: April foal by Supremacy; 5,000euros yearling; half-sister to 7f 2yo-10.5f winner Dont Do Dramas (RPR 78); dam French 10.5f-1m4f winner (including AW), half-sister to 8.6f winner Prioritise, out of 6f 2yo winning close relative of 6f 2yo Group 1 winners Blue Duster and Zieten; stable won this with a newcomer last year; worth a market check.

RaceName: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C-D) (GBB)
Spotlight: February foal by Ten Sovereigns; half-sister to winners Ombudsman (1m-10.3f including four Group 1s; RPR 133), Synchronicity (7f; 89) and American Belle (7f including AW; 83); dam 7f AW 2yo/1m2f turf winner (79), sister to 1m Group 3 winner Runnymede; stable's 2yo newcomers are always worth the closest scrutiny, while both her breeding and an entry in the Group 1 Moyglare Stud Stakes make her even more so.

RaceName: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C-D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Best of three runs on turf when second of nine at Leicester (7f, good) last month, but has since finished just under 2l behind Consultation when fifth of 13 at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm); each-way claims.

RaceName: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C-D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Sent off 16-1 when last of 11 on her Newbury debut (6f, good to firm) 12 days ago, finishing just over 4l behind Miss You Nights; looks to need more time.

RaceName: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C-D) (GBB)
Spotlight: 22-1 when only beating one home on her Yarmouth debut (6f, good to firm) last month; closely related to several AW winners and is open to improvement, but it will very much be needed.

RaceName: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C-D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Well beaten in her first two starts at Windsor (6f, good) and Brighton (7f, good to firm); breeding suggests that middle-distance handicaps will be her thing in due course.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
OwnerName: G F O'Neill & W Ward & Ms Bernadette Leigh
Spotlight: Good form last autumn, highlighted by Navan win over 2m3f (yielding) off 90; totally out of sorts in three runs since returning from a winter break in April.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Point winner two years ago; respectable run on debut at Gowran last November; given a break after two subsequent poor efforts; tongue-tie on now for handicap debut.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: All three hurdle wins have come at this venue, the last one in 2025; pulled up over C&D in May; was in the process of running a good race when falling in the lead two from home at Clonmel last month; 10yo mare could go well if suffering no ill effects.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Won a maiden on good-to-yielding over 2m3f at Limerick last October; probably not suited by testing ground in two runs before a break; encouraging run after five months off when third in an 18-runner contest at Limerick in May; stable in good form, solid chance.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Placed three times at around 2m4f; lost form after a good run at Cork 12 months ago; towards the rear in a maiden at Dowpatickm last month.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Runner-up in races at Down Royal (twice) and Downpatrick in March/April; struggled in first-time cheekpieces at Kilbeggan; same story when cheekpieces discarded for chase debut at Downpatrick; hard to be confident.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Dual hurdles winner; badly out of form last summer; back from absence when pulled up in Cork handicap hurdle; suffered the same fate over fences at Limerick.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Brought off an 80-1 shock at Kilbeggan last autumn when fitted with cheekpieces for the first time; remote seventh at Limerick early this month after a break; got a lot closer to the same winner when fifth at Kilbeggan the following week.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Weak form in maidens; pulled up in handicaps at Kilbeggan (twice) and Tramore in recent months; transformation required in first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Some improvement at Limerick last time, but still needs plenty more; cheekpieces discarded.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Returned from almost three years off when pulled up in a point in February; weakened to finish tenth of 16 at Sligo six weeks ago on his first hurdles start since 2023.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: C&D winner in May 2024; lost form after fourth placing at Limerick later that year; failed to make his presence felt over fences last season; struggled on his return to hurdling at Roscommon recently.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
OwnerName: Profit In Your Pocket Racing Club
Spotlight: Has struggled in four handicap attempts; 66-1 chance when pulled up at Sligo nine days ago.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Never a factor in four maiden hurdles; needs to step up considerably from his first handicap run at Limerick.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Sole win came at Downpatrick (2m6f, good) nearly two years ago; third at Thurles over same trip last October her best run since; down the field in two runs in May,

RaceName: Seamus Mullins Racing / Roger Jowett Memorial British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: 11,000gns foal, 25,000euros yearling, 2,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Caturra half-sister to three winners, notably Lightning Leo (7f 2yo; RPR 83); dam 1m4f/12.5f winner (91); yard has a poor strike-rate with 2yos.

RaceName: Seamus Mullins Racing / Roger Jowett Memorial British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive)
OwnerName: Whitsbury Manor Stud And Mrs M E Slade
Spotlight: Finished last of seven, about 12l behind Topaz, in 6f contest at Newmarket; may improve with the outing under her belt but has a lot to find in the rematch with that opponent.

RaceName: Seamus Mullins Racing / Roger Jowett Memorial British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: Havana Grey half-sister to two 7f winners, notably Back In Black (including 2yo; RPR 101); dam 7f 2yo winner (78), out of 6f 2yo winner; one of three newcomers in the field; check the betting.

RaceName: Seamus Mullins Racing / Roger Jowett Memorial British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: Debutante by Ten Sovereigns and first foal of once-raced half-sister to 7f Group 3 winners Dancing Star and Foxtrot Lady and granddaughter of Nunthorpe winner Lochangel; notable connections; interesting.

RaceName: Seamus Mullins Racing / Roger Jowett Memorial British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive)
Spotlight: Met a spot of trouble on her way to finishing in the back half of big field at Royal Ascot; that effort is flanked by second-place finishes, latest at Newbury (6f, good to firm); leading contender at the weights.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Ran right up to his best when fourth of 13 at Kempton (1m) two weeks ago; draw could have been kinder but he could have more to offer in blinkers.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: He has shown promise on AW, including a fourth of 13 on his stable debut in May, and two defeats on turf since are probably not the races to judge him on; still has potential on this surface.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Two wins on Lingfield's AW track this year (6f/5f) and on the same mark as for the second of them; stamina for 7f has to be confirmed.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Stayed on for third, beaten under one length, at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) 13 days ago; didn't look entirely straightforward but he holds each-way claims.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Signs of ability in his maiden runs, including C&D, but he has suffered two heavy defeats in handicaps; dropped 5lb but carries risk.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Unplaced in two Irish runs and again when 100-1 for her stable debut here last month (ridden by a 7lb claimer); this is more realistic and she needs a market check.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Prominent in the betting for her two handicap runs last month but there has been no immediate improvement; bred to be useful though and she still has time on her side.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-11 for William Knight but placed on five occasions, two of them over C&D; changed hands for 10,000gns earlier this month; each-way shout

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Two respectable efforts in handicaps last month, including C&D, without suggesting he was ahead of his mark.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Poor form over a variety of trips; hard to recommend.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Won a Punchestown handicap hurdle (2m4f, good) last October off 87; decent third on handicap chase debut at Punchestown in June; brought down in a hurdle race at Bellewstown early this month; holds a definite chance after a recent third placing at Sligo; cheekpieces may help.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Veteran was a long way out of the handicap when achieving a Punchestown win last month, the third success of a 34-race career; solid display when third at Kilbeggan off his revised mark; stays well.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Has looked badly out of form in two 2026 outings; maiden, placed twice from 15 starts.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
OwnerName: J R Robinson & Mrs E Keane
Spotlight: Easily won a 2m5f Tramore handicap hurdle off in August 2023; down the field at Punchestown after a very long absence.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Two Flat wins in 2022; finished last in two Dundalk runs after a long absence, and well held in latest three runs on turf; fifth in a Tramore handicap in 2024 was his best effort over hurdles.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Fair placed form in points; has a modest record on the track; needs to raise her game after finishing sixth at Limerick last time.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Point winner for Colin Bowe; placed in a modest maiden hurdle for Gavin Cromwell last summer; shaped promisingly when second to a fellow outsider at Bellewstown on first run for this stable; could provide the solution.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Lightly-raced mare; improved display when third over 2m at Kilbeggan last time; this trip may not be ideal.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Won a 2m6f Tramore handicap hurdle in April last year; fair run at Clonmel early last month; down the field when quite well fancied (9-2) at Limerick last time.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Beaten around 10l in seventh at Kilbeggan in her first handicap; left with quite a bit to do then; open to improvement.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Promoted to third at Limerick in May after the winner's disqualification; followed that with a fair third at Sligo; hampered and unseated at the sixth at Bellewstown; back in the money when third at Kilbeggan last time; one of the more appealing runners.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Moderate French hurdles form; unraced during a spell with Gordon Elliott; has done nothing to advertise his chance of gaining a breakthrough win.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
OwnerName: John Patrick Ryan & Miss Gillian Ryan
Spotlight: Took a step forward over hurdles when third at Clonmel in March (2m3f, soft); well below that level over fences at Tramore next time; has failed to complete his last two races; stable in form, good 5lb claimer.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Modest overall form; has failed to reach the first six in six outings in 2026; hard to be confident unless a tongue-tie makes a big difference.

RaceName: Sweeney Oil Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Maiden after 39 starts, placed 11 times; unseated in a chase on latest; not a betting proposition.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Gin Handicap
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who won at Epsom in sole outing last term; no further progress this season but retains ability and ran creditably in second-time cheekpieces at Ayr most recently; possibilities, provided the switch to blinkers has a bigger effect.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Gin Handicap
Spotlight: Justified favouritism at Salisbury (7f, good) on penultimate start and consistent otherwise this season; creditable third at Kempton (1m, AW) last time; still well treated on peak form, which includes a win off 4lb higher at Glorious Goodwood two years ago; solid.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Gin Handicap
Spotlight: Usually held up in rear; hasn't won since his 2yo days but has slipped to an attractive mark and could pop up when the cards drop right, which admittedly isn't guaranteed in this small-field scenario; less exposed at 1m than over shorter.

RaceName: Highclere Castle Gin Handicap
Spotlight: Has a record of 0-5 on turf, compared to 2-2 on AW, and isn't particularly solid on his form in this sphere (possibly flattered by best RPR); however, this combination of drop back in trip/class and first-time headgear may have a positive effect.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div II)
OwnerName: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya
Spotlight: Ran a fair race on her seasonal debut at Chelmsford in March (7f) but no progress since, including when well beaten in one run for the Frosts last month; too much to prove starting out for another yard.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Some promise on her racecourse debut last September (only run for the Muir/Grassick stable) but well beaten in three AW runs for Jonathan Portman over the winter; not a solid option starting out for another new stable after 165 days off, but worth a market check all the same.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Most of his form has been modest but he has run twice over C&D, finishing second in April and winning last month; not so good at Newcastle last time but the return to Wolverhampton should give him a lift.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div II)
OwnerName: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner
Spotlight: Finished second of ten on her handicap debut at Lingfield (6f, AW) in April; not so good twice since, including when over 5l behind Based here seven weeks ago.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Prominent racer; third of 17 at Leicester (7f, good) in April represented improvement but it hasn't been a catalyst for better things; widest draw not ideal either.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: On the up when last seen, getting off the mark at Chelmsford (7f) in February and following up over the same C&D one month later; well drawn for a front-runner but she will need to be at her best back from 131 days off.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Made all to win a C&D handicap in May off a 5lb lower mark; runner-up in three of his last four starts; usual tongue-tie dispensed with but cheekpieces are given a go.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Won a weak C&D nursery for Jack Morland last November; beaten at short odds on his next three starts and he has been well held in two runs for Tony Carroll; too much to prove for comfort.

RaceName: Book To Stay - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: C&D win in February came off a 1lb higher mark; not at his best on his last four starts and others have stronger claims.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Nathaniel half-brother was a cheap yearling; half-brother to four winners, notably Stuart Crawford's useful bumper and hurdles performer Mongibello; dam French 1m1f/10.5f winner, half-sister to French 2m5f Grade 3 hurdle winner; bar set low for debut.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Newcomer by Feels Like Dancing; first foal; dam unplaced bumpers/maiden hurdles, sister to 2m3f-3m hurdle winner Onlyfoolsownhorses, half-sister to three other hurdle winners; yard 0-35 in bumpers last five seasons.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Homebred Getaway gelding; dam placed over 1m5f, sister to yard's high-class jumper Peregrine Run and Takeyourcapoff (1m6f Flat and useful 2m-2m4f hurdle), out of bumper winner; only 11-2 when mid-division in 18-runner bumper at Punchestown; should be capable of better and anything Adam Ryan rides in bumpers is always worth a second glance.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: 25s for Navan debut last September, prominent until weakening 2f out when beaten 18l in fair race.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: 80s, much better on second start when beaten 8.5l at Kilbeggan where he looked green before staying on late; that's not hectic form, but has a place shout.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Diamond Boy half-brother to three winners in France, including Le But (1m2f-1m3f Flat and useful 2m1f-2m4f hurdle/chase); dam Flat/hurdles winner, half-sister to Eoin Griffin's smart dual-purpose mare Lounaos; no joy in two point outings.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Cost 40,000euros as 3yo; half-brother to bumper/2m hurdle winner The Model Kingdom and dam also won a bumper; smart family farther back in the pedigree as well; fancied for both points but failed to complete.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: In-form yard but 5yo has shown nothing so far in a bumper and maiden hurdle; headgear left off this time.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Has run twice in Roscommon hurdles; beaten a long way last year and early exit lately.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Homebred by Rajj; fourth foal; unraced dam is out of half-sister to Champion Hurdle winner Flakey Dove; yard only 3-86 in bumpers last five seasons.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Jet Away 5yo was only 9-1 for Kilbeggan debut; raced prominently and keenly before fading; beaten less than 7l, but the fact that only around 10l covered the first 10 home gives the form a dubious look; hood might help.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Has run in three points, a bumper and a maiden hurdle; seems modest.

RaceName: Jennings Meats INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Tailed off in heavy-ground contest at Naas before being beaten 36l at Bellewstown lately.

RaceName: Highclere Castle "The Perfect Gin" Handicap
Spotlight: Missed last season but has come back an improved filly and now bids for a 5f hat-trick after classifed/handicap wins, latest at Leicester (good to firm) seven days ago when beating Dreambird Dolly by just over 1l; respected under 5lb penalty with further progress possible.

RaceName: Highclere Castle "The Perfect Gin" Handicap
Spotlight: Gained sole success off a higher mark but isn't particularly solid on 2026 efforts, both in the spring; left Michael Attwater since; possibly best watched unless the betting speaks positively.

RaceName: Highclere Castle "The Perfect Gin" Handicap
Spotlight: Back in much better form, having won a Lingfield classified event (6f, good) in clearcut fashion last month then finished a neck second in Bath handicap (5f, firm) last week; enters calculations despite still having a modest strike-rate.

RaceName: Highclere Castle "The Perfect Gin" Handicap
OwnerName: T Beeches, C Stone, J Gallagher
Spotlight: Has a record of 2102 since wearing blinkers, the success in Leicester classified contest (maiden previously); just over 1l behind Dottie Diamond there last Tuesday and now gets a 5lb pull with that opponent, who admittedly is open to further improvement; important contender.

RaceName: Highclere Castle "The Perfect Gin" Handicap
Spotlight: Placed plenty of times since last success and current mark is attractive; well capable of taking a serious hand but his losing run is mounting up and latest effort wasn't one of his best.

RaceName: Highclere Castle "The Perfect Gin" Handicap
Spotlight: Registered sole success in AW contest in February; returned to turf with a creditable effort but subsequent form is inconsistent; never figured on debut for new yard last time.

RaceName: Highclere Castle "The Perfect Gin" Handicap
Spotlight: Has lacked consistency this term and failed to beat a rival last time, while also failing to back up his penultimate effort; yet to win away from Brighton on turf.

RaceName: Highclere Castle "The Perfect Gin" Handicap
